features.featureTitle: Extend the OrbitDock component (frontend/src/components/OrbitDock.tsx) to include a 4th dock item — Collections — alongside the existing Upload, Folders, and Mission items. The four items must be distributed symmetrically at 90° intervals on the circular orbit. The active (bottom-center) item remains the tap target; swipe gestures only rotate the orbit without opening any screen. All existing swipe behavior, infinite rotation, scale/opacity transitions, reduced-motion support, and orbital geometry must remain unchanged.

Design and implement the Collections SVG icon inside OrbitDock with a professional, minimal 2×2 grid-of-squares style (thumbnail gallery symbolism). The icon must use rounded corners and a stroke width of 2.2–2.5px. Color: warm amber #D97706 in light mode and #FBBF24 in dark mode. When inactive, the icon renders at 60% opacity, matching the visual treatment of the other dock icons.

Create a new CollectionsFullScreenView component (frontend/src/components/CollectionsFullScreenView.tsx) that opens when the Collections dock icon is tapped while active. This screen displays all uploaded items (files, notes, links) from the existing UploadContext and React Query file/note queries — the same data that already appears in the bottom-left floating stack — as an additional aggregated view. The existing bottom-left upload display must not change in any way.

In CollectionsFullScreenView, render all items in a responsive 4-column thumbnail grid. Each thumbnail cell is 80×80px on a 390px-wide mobile screen. Internal spacing between thumbnails is 8–12px. Screen-edge padding is 16px on left and right. Thumbnail corners are rounded 8–10px. Images and files render as visual previews. Notes render as card thumbnails with a note icon. Links render with a small favicon or a generic link-icon fallback. Implement lazy loading for large collections.

In CollectionsFullScreenView, reuse the existing file-selection logic from the app (single select, multi-select, select all, move to Mission, move to Folder, delete, share) exactly as implemented in GallerySection and StackFilesFullScreenView. Do not create any new selection, mutation, or action logic — wire up the existing hooks, dialogs (SendToFolderDialog, MoveToMissionDialog), and mutation calls.

Wire CollectionsFullScreenView into HomePage.tsx (frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx) using the same conditional rendering pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView. Add a showCollections state flag and pass the onCollectionsTap callback to OrbitDock. No other existing state, routing, layout, or component behavior in HomePage must change.
